title: K-200 BMS Safety Specification v1.3
text:
# K-200 BMS Safety Specification

Document number: MD-DOC-431-B. Revision: B. Type: specification. Author: Lena Okafor. Created: 2023-06-15. Last modified: 2023-07-25. Status: Released.

Reviewed by: Dr. Rhea Voss (REV-623, 2023-07-12, Approved with comments — see review for critical rationale).

## §4 Operating Limits

### §4.3 State-of-Charge Limits (per ECR-209)

The BMS module (P-1075) enforces the following SOC limits in hardware:

- **Lower SOC limit: 18%** — This floor prevents cell reversal under the 3C maximum discharge rate. March 2023 bench tests (BT-2023-03-12) showed cell reversal onset at 12% SOC. The 6-point margin (18%) was validated across the full operating temperature range (-5°C to +45°C). This limit is a hardware lockout independent of firmware. It must NOT be raised without re-running the full thermal model on the BT-2023-03-12 dataset and obtaining CSE approval via ECR process. See REV-623 comment C01 for detailed rationale from Dr. Voss.

- **Upper SOC limit: 82%** — This ceiling prevents lithium plating on the anode graphite at high SOC combined with low temperature or high charge rate. Plating was observed in post-mortem optical microscopy of test cells charged above 85% SOC at 0.5C and ambient below 5°C. The 3-point margin (82%) is required to keep the cell safely below plating onset. Implemented in hardware on P-1075. See REV-623 comment C02.

### §4.4 Emergency Disconnect

Manual disconnect via P-1064 (Battery Connector Main, AS150 gold 150A). Interrupt-under-load capability confirmed for AS150 rev B at 50A.

## §5 Temperature De-rating

### §5.2 Charge Rate De-rating vs Temperature (per ECR-209)

| Ambient temperature | Max charge rate |
|---|---|
| > 15°C | 1.0C |
| 5°C to 15°C | 0.5C |
| -5°C to 5°C | 0.3C |
| < -5°C | Charging prohibited |

Previous specification (Rev A) allowed 0.5C at -5°C to 5°C. Updated per bench test data showing plating risk at that rate/temperature combination.
